info.title: Workflow App API
info.description: Workflow applications offer non-session support and are ideal for translation, article writing, summarization AI, and more.

servers.description: The base URL for the Workflow App API. Replace {api_base_url} with the actual API base URL.

api_base_url.description: Actual base URL of the API

post.description: Execute a workflow. Cannot be executed without a published workflow.

inputs.query: Summarize this text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Translate this to French: Hello world

200.description:
Successful response. The content type and structure depend on the `response_mode` parameter in the request.

- If `response_mode` is `blocking`, returns `application/json` with a `WorkflowBlockingResponse` object.
- If `response_mode` is `streaming`, returns `text/event-stream` with a stream of `ChunkWorkflowEvent` objects.

schema.description:
A stream of Server-Sent Events (SSE). Each event is a JSON object prefixed with `data: ` and terminated by two newlines. See `ChunkWorkflowEvent` for possible event structures.

**SSE Parsing Guide:** Each event is a line prefixed with `data: ` followed by a JSON object, terminated by `\n\n`. Strip the `data: ` prefix before parsing JSON. The `event` field inside the JSON determines the event type. The stream ends when a `workflow_finished` or `error` event is received. Ignore `ping` events (sent every 10 seconds to keep the connection alive). If an `error` event is received mid-stream, the stream terminates — parse the error object for details. Note that the HTTP status code is always `200` even when an error event occurs within the stream.

streamingResponse.value: data: {"event": "workflow_started", "task_id": "c3800678-a077-43df-a102-53f23ed20b88", "workflow_run_id": "fb47b2e6-5e43-4f90-be01-d5c5a088d156", "data": {"id": "fb47b2e6-5e43-4f90-be01-d5c5a088d156", "workflow_id": "7c3e33d4-2a8b-4e5f-9b1a-d3c6e8f12345", "inputs": {"query": "Translate this"}, "created_at": 1705407629, "reason": "initial"}} data: {"event": "node_started", "task_id": "c3800678-a077-43df-a102-53f23ed20b88", "workflow_run_id": "fb47b2e6-5e43-4f90-be01-d5c5a088d156", "data": {"id": "node_exec_1", "node_id": "node_1", "node_type": "llm", "title": "LLM Node", "index": 1, "created_at": 1705407629}} data: {"event": "text_chunk", "task_id": "c3800678-a077-43df-a102-53f23ed20b88", "workflow_run_id": "fb47b2e6-5e43-4f90-be01-d5c5a088d156", "data": {"text": "Bonjour", "from_variable_selector": ["node_1", "text"]}} data: {"event": "workflow_finished", "task_id": "c3800678-a077-43df-a102-53f23ed20b88", "workflow_run_id": "fb47b2e6-5e43-4f90-be01-d5c5a088d156", "data": {"id": "fb47b2e6-5e43-4f90-be01-d5c5a088d156", "workflow_id": "7c3e33d4-2a8b-4e5f-9b1a-d3c6e8f12345", "status": "succeeded", "outputs": {"result": "Bonjour le monde"}, "elapsed_time": 1.23, "total_tokens": 150, "total_steps": 3, "created_at": 1705407629, "finished_at": 1705407630}}

400.description:
- `not_workflow_app` : App mode does not match the API route.
- `provider_not_initialize` : No valid model provider credentials found.
- `provider_quota_exceeded` : Model provider quota exhausted.
- `model_currently_not_support` : Current model unavailable.
- `completion_request_error` : Workflow execution request failed.
- `invalid_param` : Invalid parameter value.

value.message: Please check if your app mode matches the right API route.
No valid model provider credentials found. Please go to Settings -> Model Provider to complete your provider credentials.
Your quota for Dify Hosted OpenAI has been exhausted. Please go to Settings -> Model Provider to complete your own provider credentials.
Dify Hosted OpenAI trial currently not support the GPT-4 model.
Completion request failed.
Arg user must be provided.

429.description:
- `too_many_requests` : Too many concurrent requests for this app.
- `rate_limit_error` : The upstream model provider rate limit was exceeded.

value.message: Too many requests. Please try again later.
Rate Limit Error
Internal Server Error.

post.description: Execute a specific workflow version identified by its ID. Useful for running a particular published version of the workflow.

parameters.name: workflow_id
parameters.description: Workflow ID of the specific version to execute. This value is returned in the `workflow_id` field of [Run Workflow](/api-reference/workflows/run-workflow) responses and [Get Workflow Run Detail](/api-reference/workflows/get-workflow-run-detail).

200.description:
Successful response. The content type and structure depend on the `response_mode` parameter in the request.

- If `response_mode` is `blocking`, returns `application/json` with a `WorkflowBlockingResponse` object.
- If `response_mode` is `streaming`, returns `text/event-stream` with a stream of `ChunkWorkflowEvent` objects.

schema.description:
A stream of Server-Sent Events (SSE). Each event is a JSON object prefixed with `data: ` and terminated by two newlines. See `ChunkWorkflowEvent` for possible event structures.

**SSE Parsing Guide:** Each event is a line prefixed with `data: ` followed by a JSON object, terminated by `\n\n`. Strip the `data: ` prefix before parsing JSON. The `event` field inside the JSON determines the event type. The stream ends when a `workflow_finished` or `error` event is received. Ignore `ping` events (sent every 10 seconds to keep the connection alive). If an `error` event is received mid-stream, the stream terminates — parse the error object for details. Note that the HTTP status code is always `200` even when an error event occurs within the stream.

400.description:
- `not_workflow_app` : App mode does not match the API route.
- `bad_request` : Workflow is a draft or has an invalid ID format.
- `provider_not_initialize` : No valid model provider credentials found.
- `provider_quota_exceeded` : Model provider quota exhausted.
- `model_currently_not_support` : Current model unavailable.
- `completion_request_error` : Workflow execution request failed.
- `invalid_param` : Required parameter missing or invalid.

value.message: Please check if your app mode matches the right API route.
Workflow is a draft and cannot be executed.
No valid model provider credentials found. Please go to Settings -> Model Provider to complete your provider credentials.
Your quota for Dify Hosted OpenAI has been exhausted. Please go to Settings -> Model Provider to complete your own provider credentials.
Dify Hosted OpenAI trial currently not support the GPT-4 model.

get.description: Retrieve the current execution results of a workflow task based on the workflow execution ID.

parameters.name: workflow_run_id
parameters.description: Workflow run ID, can be obtained from the workflow execution response or streaming events.

400.description: `not_workflow_app` : App mode does not match the API route.

value.message: Please check if your app mode matches the right API route.
Workflow run not found.

get.description: Retrieve paginated workflow execution logs with filtering options.

parameters.name: created_at__before
parameters.description: Filter logs created before this ISO 8601 timestamp.

parameters.name: created_at__after
parameters.description: Filter logs created after this ISO 8601 timestamp.

parameters.name: created_by_end_user_session_id
parameters.description: Filter by end user session ID.

post.description: Stop a running workflow task. Only supported in `streaming` mode.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: Task ID, can be obtained from the streaming chunk return of the [Run Workflow](/api-reference/workflows/run-workflow) API.

user.description: User identifier, must be consistent with the user passed in the [Run Workflow](/api-reference/workflows/run-workflow) request.

400.description:
- `not_workflow_app` : App mode does not match the API route.
- `invalid_param` : Required parameter missing or invalid.

value.message: Please check if your app mode matches the right API route.
Arg user must be provided.

post.description: Upload a file for use when sending messages, enabling multimodal understanding of images, documents, audio, and video. Uploaded files are for use by the current end-user only.

file.description: The file to be uploaded. Supported types include images, documents, audio, and video.

user.description: User identifier, defined by the developer's rules, must be unique within the application.

400.description:
- `no_file_uploaded` : No file was provided in the request.
- `too_many_files` : Only one file is allowed per request.
- `filename_not_exists_error` : The uploaded file has no filename.

value.message: Please upload your file.
Only one file is allowed.
The specified filename does not exist.
File size exceeded.
File type not allowed.

get.description: Preview or download uploaded files previously uploaded via the [File Upload](/api-reference/files/upload-file) API. Files can only be accessed if they belong to messages within the requesting application.

parameters.name: file_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the file to preview, obtained from the [File Upload](/api-reference/files/upload-file) API response.

parameters.name: as_attachment
parameters.description: If `true`, forces the file to download as an attachment instead of previewing in browser.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: User identifier, used for end-user context.

200.description: Returns the raw file content. The `Content-Type` header is set to the file's MIME type. If `as_attachment` is `true`, the file is returned as a download with `Content-Disposition: attachment`.

403.description: `file_access_denied` : Access to the requested file is denied.

value.message: Access to the requested file is denied.

404.description: `file_not_found` : The requested file was not found.

value.message: The requested file was not found.

get.description: Retrieve an end user by ID. Useful when other APIs return an end-user ID (e.g., `created_by` from [File Upload](/api-reference/files/upload-file)).

value.message: End user not found.

post.description: Convert audio file to text. Supported formats: `mp3`, `mp4`, `mpeg`, `mpga`, `m4a`, `wav`, `webm`. File size limit is `15 MB`.

value.text: Hello, I would like to know more about the iPhone 13 Pro Max.

400.description:
- `app_unavailable` : App unavailable or misconfigured.
- `no_audio_uploaded` : No audio file was uploaded.
- `provider_not_support_speech_to_text` : Model provider does not support speech-to-text.
- `provider_not_initialize` : No valid model provider credentials found.
- `provider_quota_exceeded` : Model provider quota exhausted.
- `model_currently_not_support` : Current model does not support this operation.
- `completion_request_error` : Speech recognition request failed.

200.description: Returns the generated audio file. The `Content-Type` header is set to the audio MIME type (e.g., `audio/wav`, `audio/mp3`). If `streaming` is `true`, the audio is streamed as chunked transfer encoding.

- `completion_request_error` : Text-to-speech request failed.

get.description: Retrieve basic information about this application, including name, description, tags, and mode.
Retrieve the application's input form configuration, including feature switches, input parameter names, types, and default values.

value.opening_statement: Hello! How can I help you today?

value.message: App unavailable, please check your app configurations.

get.description: Retrieve metadata about this application, including tool icons and other configuration details.
Retrieve the WebApp settings of this application, including site configuration, theme, and customization options.

403.description: `forbidden` : Site not found for this application or the workspace has been archived.

value.message: Forbidden.

inputs.description: Key-value pairs for workflow input variables. Values for file-type variables should be arrays of file objects with `type`, `transfer_method`, and either `url` or `upload_file_id`. Refer to the `user_input_form` field in the [Get App Parameters](/api-reference/applications/get-app-parameters) response to discover the variable names and types expected by your app.

response_mode.description: Response mode. Use `blocking` for synchronous responses (Cloudflare timeout is `100 s`), or `streaming` for Server-Sent Events.

user.description: User identifier, defined by the developer's rules, must be unique within the application. This identifier scopes data access — workflow runs and files are only visible when queried with the same `user` value.

files.description: File list. Suitable when files need to be combined with text for input, available only when the model supports Vision capability. To attach a local file, first upload it via [Upload File](/api-reference/files/upload-file) and use the returned `id` as `upload_file_id` with `transfer_method: local_file`.

transfer_method.description: Transfer method: `remote_url` for file URL, `local_file` for uploaded file.

upload_file_id.description: Uploaded file ID obtained from the [Upload File](/api-reference/files/upload-file) API (when `transfer_method` is `local_file`).

task_id.description: Task ID for the in-progress execution. Use this with [Stop Workflow Task](/api-reference/workflows/stop-workflow-task) to cancel a running workflow. Only valid during execution.

workflow_run_id.description: Persistent identifier for this workflow run record. Use this with [Get Workflow Run Detail](/api-reference/workflows/get-workflow-run-detail) to retrieve results after execution.

task_id.description: Task ID for the in-progress execution. Use this with [Stop Workflow Task](/api-reference/workflows/stop-workflow-task) to cancel a running workflow. Only valid during execution.

workflow_run_id.description: Persistent identifier for this workflow run record. Use this with [Get Workflow Run Detail](/api-reference/workflows/get-workflow-run-detail) to retrieve results after execution.

inputs.description: Input variables for this workflow run.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the workflow run started.

reason.description: Reason for the workflow start. `initial` for a new execution, `resumption` when resuming from a paused state.

node_id.description: Node ID in the workflow graph.

predecessor_node_id.description: ID of the predecessor node, if any.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the node execution started.

extras.description: Additional metadata for the node execution.

iteration_id.description: Parent iteration ID if this node runs inside an iteration.

loop_id.description: Parent loop ID if this node runs inside a loop.

node_id.description: Node ID in the workflow graph.

predecessor_node_id.description: ID of the predecessor node, if any.

process_data.description: Processing data generated during node execution.

status.description: Node execution status. `running` for in-progress, `succeeded` when completed, `failed` on error, `stopped` when manually halted, `exception` on unexpected failure.

error.description: Error message if the node failed.

total_tokens.description: Total tokens consumed by this node.

total_price.description: Total cost for this node execution.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the node execution started.

finished_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the node execution finished.

iteration_id.description: Parent iteration ID if this node runs inside an iteration.

loop_id.description: Parent loop ID if this node runs inside a loop.

node_id.description: Node ID in the workflow graph.

predecessor_node_id.description: ID of the predecessor node, if any.

process_data.description: Processing data generated during node execution.

status.description: Node execution status at the retry point.

error.description: Error message that triggered the retry.

files.description: Files produced by the node during execution.

title.description: Display name of the iteration node.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the iteration started.

pre_loop_output.description: Output from the previous loop iteration.

from_variable_selector.description: Source path of the text variable in the workflow.

audio.description: Base64-encoded MP3 audio chunk. Decode and concatenate all chunks in order to produce a complete audio file.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when this audio chunk was generated.

audio.description: Empty string. Signals the end of the audio stream.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the audio stream ended.

status.description: HTTP status code of the error.

allOf.description: Keep-alive ping event. No additional properties.

status.description: Workflow execution status. `running` for in-progress executions, `succeeded` when completed successfully, `failed` when execution encountered an error, `stopped` when manually halted, `partial-succeeded` when some nodes succeeded but others failed, `paused` when awaiting human input.

error.description: Error message if the workflow failed.

total_tokens.description: Total tokens consumed across all nodes.

total_steps.description: Total number of workflow steps executed.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the workflow run was created.

finished_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the workflow run finished.

created_by.description: Creator information. Only present in streaming `workflow_finished` events.

exceptions_count.description: Number of exceptions encountered during execution. Only present in streaming `workflow_finished` events.

files.description: Files generated during workflow execution. Only present in streaming `workflow_finished` events.

status.description: Workflow execution status. `running` for in-progress executions, `succeeded` when completed successfully, `failed` when execution encountered an error, `stopped` when manually halted, `partial-succeeded` when some nodes succeeded but others failed, `paused` when awaiting human input.

inputs.description: Input variables for the workflow run.

error.description: Error message if the workflow failed.

total_steps.description: Total number of workflow steps executed.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the workflow run was created.

finished_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the workflow run finished.

created_by.description: ID of the user who uploaded the file.

type.description: End user type. Always `service_api` for Service API users.

external_user_id.description: The `user` identifier provided in API requests (e.g., the `user` field in [Send Chat Message](/api-reference/chats/send-chat-message)).

is_anonymous.description: Whether the user is anonymous. `true` when no `user` identifier was provided in the original API request.

session_id.description: Session identifier. Defaults to the `external_user_id` value.

created_by_account.description: Account details if created by an admin user.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the log entry was created.

details.description: Additional details for the log entry.

status.description: Workflow execution status. `running` for in-progress executions, `succeeded` when completed successfully, `failed` when execution encountered an error, `stopped` when manually halted, `partial-succeeded` when some nodes succeeded but others failed, `paused` when awaiting human input.

error.description: Error message if the workflow failed.

total_steps.description: Total number of workflow steps executed.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the workflow run was created.

finished_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the workflow run finished.

exceptions_count.description: Number of exceptions that occurred during execution.

triggered_from.description: Source that triggered the workflow run. `debugging` for test runs from the editor, `app` for API or app-initiated runs.

is_anonymous.description: Whether the end user is anonymous.

TextToAudioRequest.description: Request body for text-to-audio conversion. Provide either `message_id` or `text`.

message_id.description: Message ID. Takes priority over `text` when both are provided.

voice.description: Voice to use for text-to-speech. Available voices depend on the TTS provider configured for this app. Use the `voice` value from [Get App Parameters](/api-reference/applications/get-app-parameters) → `text_to_speech.voice` for the default.

mode.description: Application mode. `completion` for text generation apps, `chat` for basic chat apps, `agent-chat` for agent-based apps, `advanced-chat` for chatflow apps, `workflow` for workflow apps.

suggested_questions_after_answer.description: Configuration for suggested questions after an answer.

label.description: Display label for the input field.

variable.description: Variable name used in the workflow.

default.description: Default value for the input field.

label.description: Display label for the paragraph field.

variable.description: Variable name used in the workflow.

default.description: Default value for the paragraph field.

label.description: Display label for the select field.

variable.description: Variable name used in the workflow.

options.description: List of selectable values for this form control.

number_limits.description: Maximum number of images that can be uploaded.

detail.description: Image detail level for vision models.

transfer_methods.description: Allowed transfer methods for image upload. `remote_url` for file URL, `local_file` for uploaded file.

file_size_limit.description: Maximum general file size in MB.

image_file_size_limit.description: Maximum image file size in MB.

audio_file_size_limit.description: Maximum audio file size in MB.

video_file_size_limit.description: Maximum video file size in MB.

workflow_file_upload_limit.description: Maximum number of files per workflow execution.

background.description: Background color of the tool icon.

tool_icons.description: Tool icons. Keys are tool names.

chat_color_theme_inverted.description: Whether the chat color theme is inverted.

icon_type.description: Type of icon used. `emoji` for emoji icons, `image` for uploaded image icons.

use_icon_as_answer_icon.description: Whether to use the app icon as the answer icon.

workflow_run_id.description: Persistent identifier for this workflow run record. Use this with [Get Workflow Run Detail](/api-reference/workflows/get-workflow-run-detail) to retrieve results after execution.

error.description: Error message if the agent step failed.

display_in_ui.description: Whether to display the form in the UI.

resolved_default_values.description: Pre-resolved default values for form fields.

expiration_time.description: Unix timestamp in seconds when the form expires.
Unix timestamp when the form expired.
